title: The One with the Flashback

jokes.text: Wow, it's like a dirty math problem.
jokes.setup: Janice asks who has slept with whom among the friends
jokes.punchline: Comparing sexual history to a math problem
jokes.explanation: Classic Chandler - takes an awkward social situation and deflects with clever wordplay. Good economy and character voice.

jokes.text: Well, that's really a different question.
jokes.setup: Asked if they've ever gotten drunk and stupid
jokes.punchline: Implying they get drunk and stupid regularly, just not sexually
jokes.explanation: Good deadpan delivery distinguishing between types of drunk stupidity. Solid character moment.

jokes.text: bumped uglies
jokes.punchline: Crude but funny phrase choice
jokes.explanation: Period-appropriate crude humor. Fits Janice's character but not particularly clever.

jokes.text: There was that one time Monica and Rachel got together.
jokes.punchline: Phoebe casually suggests lesbian encounter between Monica and Rachel
jokes.explanation: Perfect Phoebe moment - delivers shocking statement with casual confidence. Creates great group reaction.

jokes.text: What? Excuse me, there was no time.
jokes.punchline: Their horrified/defensive reactions
jokes.explanation: Standard reaction humor - the payoff to Phoebe's setup. Functional but not exceptional.

jokes.text: Anybody need more coffee? - Hey, there's a dog out there!
jokes.punchline: Abrupt topic changes to escape uncomfortable moment
jokes.explanation: Good awkward moment - characters desperately changing subject. Works as tension relief.

jokes.text: Cute Naked Guy's starting to put on weight.
jokes.punchline: Casual observation about neighbor's weight gain
jokes.explanation: Typical Monica - judgmental about appearances. Character-consistent humor.

jokes.text: You didn't leave lipstick marks on the phone. Oh! Then it must have been you. Bye.
jokes.setup: Janice apologizes for lipstick on phone
jokes.punchline: Implies Chandler wears lipstick when told he didn't leave marks
jokes.explanation: Janice's oblivious logic creates funny moment. Good misdirection structure.

jokes.text: On some level, she already knows.
jokes.setup: Asked when he'll tell his sister he moved out
jokes.punchline: Vague non-answer suggesting unconscious awareness
jokes.explanation: Chandler's avoidance through pseudo-psychology. Character-appropriate deflection.

jokes.text: Okay, well, maybe not on those levels.
jokes.setup: Evidence that sister doesn't actually know he moved
jokes.punchline: Admits his previous statement was wrong
jokes.explanation: Nice callback that deflates his previous pseudo-wisdom. Self-aware humor.

jokes.text: Nice to meet you, Chandler Bing. Bing! Great apartment, Chandler Bing. Bing!
jokes.punchline: Guy who makes sound effects with Chandler's name
jokes.explanation: Absurd character detail that's memorable and quotable. Good weird energy.

jokes.text: When I answered the phone, 'Chandler Bing'... he said, 'Whoa! Short message!'
jokes.punchline: Guy thinks answering phone with full name is an answering machine message
jokes.explanation: Clever misdirection - we expect normal interaction, get absurd misunderstanding. Well-crafted joke.

jokes.text: Foot on the floor or come over no more.
jokes.punchline: Rhyming ultimatum about keeping foot on floor
jokes.explanation: Perfect Monica - controlling but in a cute rhyming way. Very quotable and character-specific.

jokes.text: Your dresser is missing, but this she notices.
jokes.punchline: Points out her selective attention to detail
jokes.explanation: Good observational humor about Monica's quirks. Well-structured complaint.

jokes.text: She met this woman at the gym, Susan something. And they really hit it off, and I think it's gonna make a difference.
jokes.punchline: Unknowingly describing his wife meeting her lover
jokes.explanation: Brilliant dramatic irony - Ross is optimistic about his marriage's destroyer. Gains power on rewatch.

jokes.text: Yes, that is cool. Uh, because I have models here, you know... never.
jokes.setup: Trying to sound cool about models
jokes.punchline: Admits he never has models over
jokes.explanation: Classic Chandler self-sabotage. Good setup/punchline structure with perfect timing.

jokes.text: Well, I still have one more person to meet. But unless it turns out to be your sister... ahem, I think your chances are pretty good.
jokes.setup: Reference to porn star sister mentioned earlier
jokes.punchline: Suggesting even a porn star would beat Chandler as roommate
jokes.explanation: Good callback that lands the insult effectively. Solid but not exceptional.

jokes.text: Bedroom, bathroom, living room. This here is the kitchen, and thanks for coming by. Bye-bye.
jokes.punchline: Ridiculously fast tour ending in immediate dismissal
jokes.explanation: Good physical comedy timing. Chandler's social awkwardness in action.

jokes.text: Ha, ha. What gay thing? Uh, just, you know, in general. The whole people-being-gay thing. Totally cool with that.
jokes.setup: Joey mentioning being okay with 'the gay thing'
jokes.punchline: Chandler's confusion and Joey's awkward clarification
jokes.explanation: Classic Joey cluelessness meeting Chandler's confusion. Good character moment.

jokes.text: Wanna hear something that sucks? - Do I ever!
jokes.setup: Monica asking if Phoebe wants to hear bad news
jokes.punchline: Phoebe's enthusiastic response to hearing bad news
jokes.explanation: Perfect Phoebe - unexpectedly enthusiastic about negativity. Great character consistency.

jokes.text: That's what I want. A roommate I can walk around with... and be referred to as 'the funny one.'
jokes.setup: Commenting on attractive Italian roommate candidate
jokes.punchline: Self-aware about becoming the less attractive friend
jokes.explanation: Excellent self-aware humor that many can relate to. Well-structured and very quotable.

jokes.text: Get ready for me to whip your butt. Okay, but after that we're shooting some pool.
jokes.punchline: Phoebe's sexual misinterpretation
jokes.explanation: Good double entendre with perfect timing. Classic misdirection structure.

jokes.text: How hard is it to get a couple drinks right, huh?
jokes.punchline: Being demanding about service
jokes.explanation: Shows Rachel's entitled personality but not particularly funny on its own.

jokes.text: Isn't it exciting? I mean, it's like having a boyfriend for life! Heh!
jokes.punchline: Unromantic description of marriage as permanent boyfriend
jokes.explanation: Shows Rachel's immature view of marriage. Mildly amusing character moment.

jokes.text: I really think I need to just have... some meaningless sex, you know? With the next guy I see.
jokes.punchline: Declaring she'll sleep with next guy she sees
jokes.explanation: Sets up the upcoming encounter with Ross. Functional setup for bigger payoff.

jokes.text: Excuse me, I seem to have dropped my ball. Yeah, so? And now I've picked it up again.
jokes.punchline: Rachel's dismissive response to his obvious pickup attempt
jokes.explanation: Good cringe moment showing Ross's awkward pickup skills. Rachel's dismissal is perfect.

jokes.text: You can't even see where the Titanic hit it.
jokes.punchline: Comparing ring's size to the Titanic disaster
jokes.explanation: Clever metaphor about ring size using historical disaster. Very quotable and unexpected.

jokes.text: Ten bucks says I never see that woman again in my life.
jokes.punchline: Prediction she'll never see her again (dramatic irony for audience)
jokes.explanation: Dramatic irony - audience knows Rachel becomes main character. Great on rewatch.

jokes.text: What are they doing? - I don't know. Something girly.
jokes.setup: Asked about Carol and Susan's plans
jokes.punchline: Dismissive masculine response
jokes.explanation: Generic male response to female activities. Not particularly clever but character-appropriate.

jokes.text: You don't play the oboe. - I could play the oboe.
jokes.setup: Heckles claiming oboe practice is being disturbed
jokes.punchline: His illogical defense when caught lying
jokes.explanation: Perfect Heckles moment - absurd logic delivered with conviction. Great character work.

jokes.text: I'm Eric, Chandler's new roommate. - I'm Chandler's new roommate. - I don't think so. - I could be Chandler's new roommate.
jokes.setup: Two people claiming to be Chandler's roommate
jokes.punchline: Heckles inserting himself with same illogical 'I could be' structure
jokes.explanation: Excellent callback that escalates the absurdity. Perfect Heckles character moment.

jokes.text: It's the heat. And the humidity. That's... a tough combination.
jokes.punchline: Stating the obvious about weather with fake confidence
jokes.explanation: Good awkward flirting moment. Ross trying to sound knowledgeable but stating obvious.

jokes.text: So if the landlord ever asks... I'm an 87-year-old woman who's afraid of her VCR.
jokes.punchline: Specific absurd cover story about being elderly and technophobic
jokes.explanation: Very specific and absurd detail that's memorable. Good escalation from simple lie.

jokes.text: Okay... here's your penis!
jokes.punchline: Ross has stripped naked expecting sex
jokes.explanation: Shock value works well here. Good physical comedy setup with strong reaction.

jokes.text: Usually, yeah. Well, not just lemonade... iced tea, sometimes juice.
jokes.punchline: Expanding to other beverages as sexual invitations
jokes.explanation: Good escalation that makes the misunderstanding more absurd. Adds layers to the joke.

jokes.text: By the way, I can still see it.
jokes.punchline: Monica's matter-of-fact observation about his exposure
jokes.explanation: Perfect deadpan delivery caps off the physical comedy. Monica's bluntness works well.

jokes.text: You could've just turned the cushion over. I would have, but I had a spaghetti stain on the other side.
jokes.setup: Monica's obsession with ink stain keeping her awake
jokes.punchline: Reveals both sides of cushion are stained
jokes.explanation: Perfect Monica moment showing her neurotic perfectionism. Good escalation structure.

jokes.text: I need to live in a land where people can spill! - Well, you can spill. In the sink.
jokes.setup: Phoebe's dramatic plea for freedom to spill
jokes.punchline: Monica's literal interpretation limiting spilling to sink
jokes.explanation: Exceptional character work - both voices perfectly captured. Monica's control freak nature perfectly displayed.

jokes.text: No, you're not. You're wondering which cushion it is.
jokes.punchline: Phoebe correctly identifying Monica's real concern
jokes.explanation: Brilliant character insight - Phoebe knows Monica so well. Perfect callback to cushion obsession.

jokes.text: No... You don't have a boyfriend because... I don't know why you don't have a boyfriend. You should have a boyfriend.
jokes.setup: Monica asking why she doesn't have a boyfriend
jokes.punchline: Ross's fumbling non-answer that becomes sweetly supportive
jokes.explanation: Good awkward moment that shows Ross's genuine care beneath the fumbling. Character-driven.

jokes.text: Listen, you are one of my favorite people... and the most beautiful woman I've ever known in real life.
jokes.punchline: Qualifier 'in real life' undermines the compliment
jokes.explanation: Classic Ross - ruins romantic moment with awkward qualifier. Good character consistency.

jokes.text: Because Carol's a lesbian. And I'm not one. And apparently it's not a mix-and-match situation.
jokes.setup: Asked why his marriage is over
jokes.punchline: Describing sexual incompatibility in retail terms
jokes.explanation: Excellent joke - painful situation described in hilariously mundane terms. Very quotable and well-crafted.

jokes.text: And I'd think: 'God, my wife is cool.' Heh.
jokes.punchline: Ross's naive interpretation of obvious signs
jokes.explanation: Great dramatic irony - Ross completely missing obvious signs. Painful but funny.

jokes.text: Well, now I do!
jokes.setup: Phoebe asking if Susan is Carol's lover
jokes.punchline: Ross's delayed realization
jokes.explanation: Good reaction beat showing Ross's dawning understanding. Solid callback.

jokes.text: Is it 100 percent cotton?
jokes.punchline: Monica focusing on fabric content instead of emotion
jokes.explanation: Perfect Monica moment - practical concerns override romance. Excellent character-specific humor.

jokes.text: Maybe this wouldn't have happened if I'd been more nurturing... or I'd paid more attention... or I had a uterus.
jokes.setup: Ross analyzing what went wrong with marriage
jokes.punchline: Escalates from reasonable to absurd with uterus comment
jokes.explanation: Good escalation from realistic self-doubt to absurd anatomical concerns. Quotable.

jokes.text: Wait, wait, wait! My foot is stuck in the pocket.
jokes.setup: Ross and Monica about to have sex
jokes.punchline: Monica's foot trapped in pool table pocket
jokes.explanation: Good physical comedy that interrupts romantic moment. Creates awkward pause in escalation.

jokes.text: That's not something a girl wants to hear.
jokes.setup: Ross saying he can't get it out
jokes.punchline: Monica's dry observation about sexual implications
jokes.explanation: Good double entendre awareness. Monica's practical response to awkward situation.

jokes.text: My wife's a lesbian. Cool!
jokes.punchline: Joey's enthusiastic misunderstanding of the situation
jokes.explanation: Perfect Joey response - completely missing the emotional context. Great character establishment.

jokes.text: You missed the exit. Oh! Sorry. My God, what were you thinking about? Ahem, um... Barry.
jokes.punchline: Claims thinking about fiancé while actually thinking about Ross encounter
jokes.explanation: Good lie to cover up what really happened. Shows Rachel's internal conflict.

tmdb_overview: When Janice asks the gang if any of them have ever slept together, the friends flash back to a time three years before.
